authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations
sources.quotedText: Every body of water listed in subsection (b) is closed to all fishing except during the open season as shown. Unless otherwise provided, waters shown as open to trout fishing in subsection (b), are open to fishing for other species.

sources.title: 14 CCR §5.85 — Trout, General Statewide Regulations
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Trout Season 2026 Opens April 25 Across State
sources.quotedText: Most lakes, rivers and streams have a limit of five trout per day and a 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: 14 CCR §5.00 — Black Bass, General Statewide Restrictions
sources.quotedText: Lakes/Reservoirs and the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta: The following waters, except for those listed in subsection (b) Special Regulations (below), are open to fishing all year, with a 12-inch total length minimum size limit and a five-fish daily bag limit: All lakes and reservoirs in the State...

sources.title: CDFW — Fishing for Kokanee (14 CCR §5.41)
sources.quotedText: 5.41. LANDLOCKED SALMON (a) Open season: All year (b) Daily bag limit: Five (c) Possession limit: Ten (d) Size limit: None

sources.title: CDFW — Get a Fishing or Hunting License (Fish & Game Code §7145)
sources.quotedText: Any person who is 16 years of age or older must possess a valid sport fishing license when taking any fish, shell fish, reptile, or amphibian in California according to Fish and Game Code Section 7145.

sources.title: California Fish & Game Code §7145
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: California Fish & Game Code §7149.4 — Second-Rod Sport Fishing Stamp
sources.quotedText: Any person who has a valid second-rod sport fishing stamp affixed to his or her valid sport fishing license may fish with two rods in inland lakes and reservoirs in any sport fishery in which the regulations of the commission provide for the taking of fish by angling.

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (CDFW, updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: Anyone 16 years or older fishing anywhere in Lake Tahoe or Topaz Lake must possess either a California sport fishing license or a Nevada sport fishing license (Nevada also requires a trout stamp).

sources.title: Wikipedia — Donner Lake, Nevada County, California (infobox coordinates; used because the USGS GNIS ArcGIS gazetteer (nationalmap.gov geonames MapServer/7) has no queryable record for this lake by name or by gaz_id in Nevada County, CA — confirmed by direct gaz_id and bounding-box queries against the service)
sources.quotedText: 39°19′22″N 120°15′52″W﻿ / ﻿39.32278°N 120.26444°W = 39.32278, -120.26444 — corrected from the file's prior recorded coordinate (-120.2437, 39.3237), which was off by ~1.8km (near the lake's east shore/outlet rather than its center). Wikidata (Q3888447) cross-references GNIS Feature ID 1654915 for this lake; the OSM/Nominatim relation centroid (39.3229, -120.2691) and latitude.to (39.3203, -120.2587) independently corroborate this location.

regulations.humanSummary: Open to trout fishing all year — Donner Lake is not on CCR §7.50(b)'s special-regulations list, so the statewide default for lakes and reservoirs applies (no closed season).
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout per day, 10 in possession — the statewide default limit for California lakes and reservoirs (rainbow, brown, golden, and cutthroat trout combined).
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 kokanee salmon per day, 10 in possession — Donner Lake is not among the reduced-limit exception waters (Bucks, Pardee, New Bullards Bar, Trinity), so the statewide landlocked-salmon default applies. Counted separately from the trout limit.
regulations.verbatimText: 5.41. LANDLOCKED SALMON (a) Open season: All year (b) Daily bag limit: Five (c) Possession limit: Ten (d) Size limit: None

regulations.humanSummary: 5 black bass per day — statewide default for California lakes and reservoirs. Smallmouth bass presence at Donner Lake is reported by fishing-data sites but not directly confirmed via a CDFW stocking/survey page — treat species presence as medium confidence.
regulations.verbatimText: Lakes/Reservoirs and the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta: The following waters, except for those listed in subsection (b) Special Regulations (below), are open to fishing all year, with a 12-inch total length minimum size limit and a five-fish daily bag limit: All lakes and reservoirs in the State...

regulations.humanSummary: 12-inch total-length minimum for black bass — statewide default for lakes and reservoirs.
regulations.verbatimText: ...open to fishing all year, with a 12-inch total length minimum size limit and a five-fish daily bag limit: All lakes and reservoirs in the State...

regulations.humanSummary: Anglers 16 and older must carry a valid California sport fishing license.
regulations.verbatimText: Any person who is 16 years of age or older must possess a valid sport fishing license when taking any fish, shell fish, reptile, or amphibian in California according to Fish and Game Code Section 7145.

parameters.requires_permit_note: A valid second-rod stamp is required to fish with a second rod.

regulations.humanSummary: No bait or lure restrictions. Anglers may fish with up to 2 rods at once if they hold a valid second-rod stamp.
regulations.verbatimText: Any person who has a valid second-rod sport fishing stamp affixed to his or her valid sport fishing license may fish with two rods in inland lakes and reservoirs in any sport fishery in which the regulations of the commission provide for the taking of fish by angling.

reciprocity.note: CDFW's official regulations booklet names only Lake Tahoe and Topaz Lake as waters where a Nevada license is honored in place of a California license. Donner Lake is not on that list, so a Nevada license is NOT honored there — anglers 16+ need a valid California sport fishing license.
